question_title: Will France have a cabinet reshuffle replacing more than two ministers before May 1, 2026?
summary: The French cabinet reshuffle has already occurred within the resolution window. JORF decrees dated February 22 and February 26, 2026 document at least 3 minister-level replacements: (1) Amélie de Montchalin replaced by David Amiel (Public Accounts, Feb 22), (2) Rachida Dati replaced by Catherine Pégard (Culture, Feb 26), and (3) Charlotte Parmentier-Lecocq replaced by Camille Galliard-Minier (Disabilities, Feb 26). All fall within the Feb 17 – May 1, 2026 window. Three replacements exceeds the "more than two" threshold. This is confirmed by the exact resolution source (JORF) and corroborated by multiple credible sources including vie-publique.fr, franceinfo, and others.

factors.description: JORF decree of Feb 22, 2026 confirms Amiel replaced Montchalin as minister for public accounts — 1st replacement confirmed via official resolution source
JORF decree of Feb 26, 2026 confirms Pégard replaced Dati (Culture) and Galliard-Minier replaced Parmentier-Lecocq (Disabilities) — 2nd and 3rd replacements confirmed
All decrees fall within Feb 17 – May 1, 2026 window as required by resolution criteria
Multiple credible sources (vie-publique.fr, franceinfo, Connexion France, Gulf News) corroborate the same facts
Small residual risk: interpretation dispute about whether some replacements qualify (e.g., new positions vs replacements of prior holders)

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.97 (YES)**

The French cabinet reshuffle has already occurred. Two JORF decrees (Feb 22 and Feb 26, 2026) document at least 3 minister-level person-replacements within the resolution window:

1. **Amélie de Montchalin → David Amiel** (Public Accounts, Feb 22 decree)
2. **Rachida Dati → Catherine Pégard** (Culture, Feb 26 decree)
3. **Charlotte Parmentier-Lecocq → Camille Galliard-Minier** (Disabilities, Feb 26 decree)

All three are clear person-for-person replacements at minister/ministre déléguée level, documented via the exact resolution source (JORF on Legifrance), and dated within the Feb 17 – May 1 window. The 3% residual accounts for extremely unlikely interpretation disputes.

[French government reshuffle: who is in and who is out](https://www.connexionfrance.com/news/reshuffle-who-is-in-and-who-is-out-in-new-french-government/774026)
[Remaniement : la composition du gouvernement de Sébastien Lecornu au 26 février 2026](https://www.vie-publique.fr/en-bref/302256-remaniement-le-gouvernement-de-sebastien-lecornu-26-fevrier-2026)
[Décret du 26 février 2026 relatif à la composition du Gouvernement](https://www.legifrance.gouv.fr/jorf/id/JORFTEXT000053586369)
resolution_reason: A cabinet reshuffle occurred on February 26, 2026 (decree published in JORF on February 27, 2026), which falls within the resolution period (Feb 17 - May 1, 2026). Three individuals were replaced at minister-level roles: (1) Rachida Dati replaced by Catherine Pégard as Minister of Culture, (2) Amélie de Montchalin replaced by David Amiel as Minister of Public Accounts, and (3) Charlotte Parmentier-Lecocq replaced by Camille Galliard-Minier as Ministre déléguée for Autonomy and Persons with Disabilities. This exceeds the threshold of "more than two" replacements required for YES resolution.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the Journal officiel de la République française (JORF) publishes appointment and/or termination decrees with dates on or after February 17, 2026 and before May 1, 2026 that result in more than two individuals being replaced in roles at the level of ministre, ministre délégué, or secrétaire d’État (counting replacements of prior holders). This question resolves to no otherwise. The primary source is Legifrance’s Journal officiel portal at https://www.legifrance.gouv.fr/jorf/jo
fine_print: Count only changes that replace the person holding a minister-level role (not changes limited to attributions, titles, or internal reorganizations without a change of person). If multiple decrees occur on different dates, aggregate replacements across the full period on or after February 17, 2026 and before May 1, 2026. If disputes arise about whether a role qualifies, resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions while prioritizing JORF decrees. Candidate Sources: Élysée “Composition du Gouvernement” page https://www.elysee.fr/le-gouvernement
background_info:
Large reshuffles are a strong signal of political instability or policy reset.
